subject: english
topic_title: Revision of Tenses and Narrative: My Holiday Experience
learning_objectives: Indicate the time of an action using past, present, and future tenses
Differentiate between regular and irregular verbs
Identify the features and outline of a narrative essay

sections.heading: Grammar: Tenses
sections.content: Tense is a term which applies to verbs, and it is used to indicate the time of an action: past, present, future tense. These are the basic categories of tenses we have in English. The Past Tense – is used to express a past action. This is indicated in most verbs by adding ‘ed’ or ‘d’ to them. This type of verbs are called the regular verbs. Example of such verbs are: voted, waited, counted, preached, visited, cured, answered, elected, abrogated, punched, perfected etc. verbs which do not take ‘ed’ or ‘d’ to form their past are called irregular verbs e.g. built, threw, said, paid wore, sent, swam, set, cast, ran, wrote etc. The Present Tense – is used to express the current time i.e. now. This tense could be singular or plural and it must agree with the subject in a sentence. Examples are vote, wait, say, count, preach, elect, answer(v), remove, empower, tolerate, activate etc. The Future Tense – This is done by putting an auxiliary before the verb and so on. Examples are as follows. Will + go e.g. I will go tomorrow. Shall + visit e.g. I shall visit you next week Be + going e.g. I am travelling next year. Be + -ing verb e.g. She is coming next month Be + about to + verb e.g. Theyare about to start the lecture. ‘s’ i.e. progressive form of verbs e.g. the bus leaves tonight.

sections.heading: Narrative: My Holiday Experience
sections.content: Features: As a narrative, use the past tense. Arrange your events one after the other. Use connectors/transitional expressions. Raise suspense. Describe people, places and events. Use figurative language, proverbs or idioms. Outline: Write your title boldly in capital letters (preferably). Your introduction should be interesting. Indicate when the holidays started. Describe how you had felt at the prospect of a holiday. Mention where and with whom you spent the holiday. Disclose what happened and your reactions. Mention the interesting parts and annoying ones. Reveal the lessons learnt.
